title: Near-sunflower analogue of the Erdős-Rado Sunflower Conjecture
statement_text: If $\mathcal{F}$ is a $k$-uniform family of sets containing no near-sunflower of size $r$, then $|\mathcal{F}| \leq C^k$, where $C$ is a constant depending only on $r$.
context_text: The paper introduces near-sunflowers as a weakening of sunflowers (allowing elements to belong to $0, 1, r-1,$ or $r$ sets in the family) and proposes this bound as a weaker version of the notorious Erdős-Rado sunflower conjecture. While the paper resolves the Erdős-Szemerédi-type question (exponential bound in $n$) for near-sunflowers via Theorem 1.3, the Erdős-Rado-type question (exponential bound in $k$) is explicitly left open; Section 6 proves the bound only under a stronger condition.
notes: Section 6, where this conjecture is formally stated, is truncated in the provided PDF extraction; statement reconstructed from the abstract and introduction. PDF source — math notation may be garbled.

title: Informal conjecture on non-tightness of upper bound for binary focal families
statement_text: For $q = 2$ and large $n$, the upper bound $g^{q\text{-ff}}_r(n) \leq (r-1)\, q^{\lceil (r-2)n/(r-1) \rceil}$ of Theorem 1.3 is not tight.
context_text: Proposition 3.2 shows via Reed-Solomon codes that the upper bound is essentially tight when $q \geq n$ and $q$ is a prime power. The authors believe the binary case ($q = 2$) behaves differently, and support this belief by proving in Section 4 that the bound can be significantly improved when the family of binary vectors forms a linear code (closed under addition modulo 2).
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled.
